estimate_rationale: Sum of per-feature estimated_lines (031..045). Framework-side of handover NIP-0001 (F-001..F-015): one NIP + planning activation, one module index, four protocol docs, three JSON schemas (each with positive + negative fixtures), capability routing across NIZAM.json/tools, one deterministic preflight CLI, a validator + CI fixtures extension, two dogfood-evidence features, and a human-gated release. Docs and schemas dominate; the CLI (041) and validator extension (042) carry the largest code deltas. Consumer adoption (F-016..F-020) is excluded from this figure as successor-phase scope.

provenance.out_of_scope: Handover F-016..F-020 (consumer adoption) deferred to the successor programme phase.

features.name: NIP-0001 Acceptance + Phase-005 Plan of Record (PR-F1)
features.description: Handover F-001 (Accept NIP-0001). Land the accepted NIP-0001 and the phase-005 plan of record: docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md with body status Accepted and an acceptance record (operator, 2026-07-17, verbatim authorization); the phase-005 planning artifacts (.agent/product_spec_005.md, .agent/feature_list_005.json); the manifest phase-005 activation entry; and the ROADMAP update marking phase 005 the active plan of record. Frontmatter status is 'active' (frontmatter schema enum is draft/active/deprecated); the decision-lifecycle state 'Accepted' is carried in the body, matching the docs/architecture/ADR house pattern. No ecosystem module/schema/tooling work in this feature.
acceptance_tests: NIP exists with status accepted after operator approval
NIP paths and frontmatter validate
NIP file exists: test -f docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md

NIP frontmatter validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))"

NIP body records the Accepted decision, the operator, the 2026-07-17 date, and the verbatim authorization: python3 -c "t=open('docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md',encoding='utf-8').read(); import sys; sys.exit(0 if ('Accepted' in t and '2026-07-17' in t and 'approved. expedite.' in t) else 1)"

manifest.json indexes phase 005 with its feature list path and current_phase points at it: python3 -c "import json,sys; m=json.load(open('docs/planning/manifest.json')); ids=[p['id'] for p in m['phases']]; ok=any(i.startswith('005') for i in ids) and m['current_phase'].startswith('005'); sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)"

manifest.json still validates against schema/manifest.schema.json: python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; jsonschema.validate(json.load(open('docs/planning/manifest.json')), json.load(open('schema/manifest.schema.json')))"

feature_list_005.json validates against schema/feature_list.schema.json: python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; jsonschema.validate(json.load(open('.agent/feature_list_005.json')), json.load(open('schema/feature_list.schema.json')))"

[AMENDED 2026-07-17 per contract 031 rev1 / qa/031-contract-review.json: the original command `grep -Eqi '005' docs/planning/ROADMAP.md` was a reproduced false-pass at HEAD (six pre-existing 'phase 005 candidate scope' references); superseded by contract-031 verification entries 7-8, which require the literal 'Plan of Record (2026-07-17)' banner AND the Track-2-heading-anchored SUPERSEDED marker, with an anti-vacuity companion proving the check fails at HEAD] ROADMAP.md marks phase 005 as the active plan of record (not merely candidate scope): grep -Eqi '005' docs/planning/ROADMAP.md
Existing compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Ecosystem Module Index (PR-F2)
features.description: Handover F-002 (Create ecosystem module index). Complete ecosystem/README.md so it defines the canonical Bootstrap->Preflight->Baseline->Audit->Plan->Execute->Verify->Promote->Compare->Repeat lifecycle and navigates the module's protocol documents, and index the ecosystem module in NIZAM.json. The existing 12-line ecosystem/README.md stub on the branch is completed here (not in this planning feature).
acceptance_tests: ecosystem/README.md defines lifecycle and module navigation
NIZAM.json indexes the module

ecosystem/README.md names the full canonical lifecycle: python3 -c "t=open('ecosystem/README.md',encoding='utf-8').read().lower(); import sys; sys.exit(0 if all(k in t for k in ['preflight','baseline','audit','compare']) else 1)"

ecosystem/README.md frontmatter validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('ecosystem/README.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))"

[AMENDED 2026-07-17 per contract 032 rev1 / qa/032-contract-review.json + qa/032.json: the literal grep command is a reproduced vacuous match (pre-existing 'ecosystem' substrings in NIZAM.json prose, confirmed still vacuous post-implementation); the enforced gate is contract-032 verification entry 13 (exactly one path=='ecosystem' module object with key_documents exactly ['ecosystem/README.md']) with anti-vacuity companion entry 14] NIZAM.json indexes the ecosystem module and every indexed path resolves (C4-green): grep -q 'ecosystem' NIZAM.json && bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json; test $? -eq 0
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Clean-State Preflight Protocol (PR-F2)
features.description: Handover F-003 (Define clean-state preflight protocol). Author ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md defining the reusable preflight protocol and its machine-readable verdict: exactly one of PASS / PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS / FAIL, with explicit blocking rules and the operator-exception rule (PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS requires explicit operator approval before execution continues).
acceptance_tests: Protocol defines PASS/PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS/FAIL
Blocking and operator-exception rules are explicit

The protocol doc names all three verdicts and the operator-exception rule: python3 -c "t=open('ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md',encoding='utf-8').read(); import sys; sys.exit(0 if all(k in t for k in ['PASS','PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS','FAIL']) and ('operator' in t.lower()) else 1)"

Frontmatter validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))"
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Immutable Baseline Protocol (PR-F2)
features.description: Handover F-004 (Define immutable baseline protocol). Author ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md defining a point-in-time baseline over framework, repository, dependency, CI, planning, and evidence references, with explicit fields and evidence rules. A baseline MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions (every fact is anchored to a stated SHA/timestamp).
acceptance_tests: Baseline fields and evidence rules are defined
Baseline cannot mix evidence from unspecified revisions

The protocol doc states baseline fields and the revision/timestamp anchoring rule: python3 -c "t=open('ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md',encoding='utf-8').read().lower(); import sys; sys.exit(0 if ('baseline' in t and ('revision' in t or 'sha' in t) and ('timestamp' in t or 'point-in-time' in t)) else 1)"

Frontmatter validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))"
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Engineering Audit Protocol (PR-F2)
features.description: Handover F-005 (Define engineering audit protocol). Author ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md defining an evidence-first engineering audit that uses the evidence hierarchy and the ten-state maturity model (Designed..Production Proven) with confidence. No claim may be promoted beyond its evidence. Commercial/GA readiness MUST NOT be embedded in generic engineering scoring (kept to the separate GA gate).
acceptance_tests: Audit uses evidence hierarchy and maturity model
Commercial readiness is not embedded in generic engineering scoring

The protocol doc references the maturity model and the no-claim-beyond-evidence rule: python3 -c "t=open('ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md',encoding='utf-8').read().lower(); import sys; sys.exit(0 if ('maturity' in t and 'evidence' in t and 'confidence' in t) else 1)"

Frontmatter validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))"
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Progress Comparison Protocol (PR-F2)
features.description: Handover F-006 (Define progress comparison protocol). Author ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md defining how two approved baselines or audits are compared: the comparison distinguishes new / resolved / reopened / stale findings, and every score movement is traceable to evidence (resolved findings close only with closure evidence; stale evidence is not silently reused).
acceptance_tests: Comparison distinguishes new/resolved/reopened/stale findings
Score movement is traceable to evidence

The protocol doc names the four finding-transition classes: python3 -c "t=open('ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md',encoding='utf-8').read().lower(); import sys; sys.exit(0 if all(k in t for k in ['new','resolved','reopened','stale']) else 1)"

Frontmatter validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))"
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Baseline Schema + Fixtures (PR-F2)
features.description: Handover F-007 (Add baseline schema). Add schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json describing the immutable baseline artifact (framework/repository/dependency/CI/planning/evidence references with revision anchors), index it in NIZAM.json, and add positive + negative fixtures under tools/fixtures/. The positive fixture validates; the negative fixture (e.g. baseline mixing states without timestamps) fails.
acceptance_tests: Positive fixture validates
Negative fixture fails
The schema is itself a valid JSON Schema: python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema(json.load(open('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json')))"

A positive baseline fixture validates AND a negative baseline fixture is rejected: python3 -c "import json,jsonschema,sys; s=json.load(open('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json'))
import glob
pos=[f for f in glob.glob('tools/fixtures/*baseline*') if 'valid' in f or 'pos' in f]
neg=[f for f in glob.glob('tools/fixtures/*baseline*') if 'invalid' in f or 'neg' in f]
assert pos and neg, 'need positive and negative baseline fixtures'
jsonschema.validate(json.load(open(pos[0])), s)
try:
  jsonschema.validate(json.load(open(neg[0])), s); sys.exit(1)
except jsonschema.ValidationError:
  sys.exit(0)"
NIZAM.json indexes the new schema and stays C4-green: grep -q 'ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' NIZAM.json && bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json; test $? -eq 0
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Preflight Verdict Schema + Fixtures (PR-F2)
features.description: Handover F-008 (Add preflight verdict schema). Add schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json supporting exactly the three verdicts (PASS / PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS / FAIL) and requiring a structured operator-approval state whenever the verdict is PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS. Index it in NIZAM.json and add positive + negative fixtures (a fixture with an unsupported verdict, and a PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS fixture lacking operator-approval metadata, are rejected).
acceptance_tests: Three verdicts supported
Exceptions require structured approval state

The schema is itself a valid JSON Schema and constrains the verdict to the three values: python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; s=json.load(open('schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json')); jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema(s); assert 'PASS' in json.dumps(s) and 'PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS' in json.dumps(s) and 'FAIL' in json.dumps(s)"
NIZAM.json indexes the new schema and stays C4-green: grep -q 'preflight_verdict.schema.json' NIZAM.json && bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json; test $? -eq 0
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Engineering Finding Schema + Fixtures (PR-F2)
features.description: Handover F-009 (Add engineering finding schema). Add schema/engineering_finding.schema.json supporting severity, confidence, evidence, impact, owner, and closure criteria for repository and ecosystem findings. Index it in NIZAM.json and add positive + negative fixtures (e.g. a finding marked resolved without closure evidence is rejected).
acceptance_tests: Severity, confidence, evidence, impact, owner, closure criteria supported
Positive and negative fixtures exist

The schema is itself a valid JSON Schema and names the required finding fields: python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; s=json.load(open('schema/engineering_finding.schema.json')); jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema(s); d=json.dumps(s).lower(); assert all(k in d for k in ['severity','confidence','evidence','impact','owner'])"
NIZAM.json indexes the new schema and stays C4-green: grep -q 'engineering_finding.schema.json' NIZAM.json && bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json; test $? -eq 0
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Register Ecosystem Capabilities (PR-F2)
features.description: Handover F-010 (Register ecosystem capabilities). Register the ecosystem lifecycle capabilities so agents can discover and route them: NIZAM.json paths resolve, tools/skill.json routes the capabilities, and tools/SKILL.md remains a router (not a mirror of the protocol bodies). The single runtime-agnostic skill stays the router; no runtime-specific ecosystem skills are introduced.
acceptance_tests: NIZAM.json paths resolve
tools/skill.json routes capabilities
tools/SKILL.md remains a router rather than a mirror
NIZAM.json stays C4-green with every indexed path resolving: bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json; test $? -eq 0

tools/skill.json parses and routes an ecosystem capability whose module path resolves on disk: python3 -c "import json,os,sys; s=json.load(open('tools/skill.json')); caps=s.get('capabilities',s); js=json.dumps(s); sys.exit(0 if 'ecosystem' in js.lower() else 1)"
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Minimum Preflight CLI (PR-F3)
features.description: Handover F-011 (Implement minimum preflight CLI). Add tools/ecosystem_preflight.py, the minimum deterministic preflight tool: it emits schema-valid preflight and baseline artifacts, supports a framework-self fixture mode (so the framework can dogfood against itself), and documents its failure exit codes. Composes evidence externalisation per the framework's tool-driven-state convention.
acceptance_tests: CLI emits schema-valid preflight and baseline artifacts
CLI supports framework-self fixture mode
Failure exit codes are documented
The CLI exists and is syntactically valid Python: test -f tools/ecosystem_preflight.py && python3 -m py_compile tools/ecosystem_preflight.py

Run in framework-self fixture mode emits a preflight artifact that validates against schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json and a baseline artifact that validates against schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json (evidence captured by path under .agent/evidence/)

Failure exit codes are documented in the CLI (help text or module docstring): python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --help 2>&1 | grep -Eqi 'exit|code|fail' || python3 -c "import ast,sys; t=ast.get_docstring(ast.parse(open('tools/ecosystem_preflight.py').read())); sys.exit(0 if t and ('exit' in t.lower()) else 1)"
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Extend Validator + CI Fixtures (PR-F3)
features.description: Handover F-012 (Extend validator and CI fixtures). Extend the framework's validation to cover the new ecosystem schema families (baseline / preflight-verdict / finding) with positive AND negative fixtures that prove the checks are load-bearing, wired so the existing C1-C11 and bootstrap tests continue to pass. Any new validate.sh check migrates the SUMMARY count deterministically and is proven non-breaking against the current tree first (the phase-004 C-check precedent).
acceptance_tests: New schema families are validated
Negative fixtures prove checks are load-bearing
Existing C1-C11 and bootstrap tests pass
Each new ecosystem schema family has a negative fixture that its validation rejects (non-zero exit) via the fixture-driver / --target path

The full validator sweep passes at its (deterministically migrated) count and exits 0: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); test $? -eq 0; echo "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: [0-9]+ passed, 0 failed'
No pre-existing check regressed: for c in C1 C2 C3 C4 C5 C6 C7 C8 C9 C10 C11; do bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | grep -q "\[$c\] PASS" || exit 1; done
The hermetic e2e bootstrap test still passes: bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh >/dev/null 2>&1; test $? -eq 0

features.name: Dogfood Framework Preflight (PR-F4)
features.description: Handover F-013 (Dogfood framework preflight). Run the new preflight against nizam-framework as a one-repository ecosystem: generate a framework baseline, record the preflight verdict, store all evidence by path (.agent/reconciliation/<id>/preflight.json, .agent/reconciliation/<id>/baseline.json, .agent/evidence/<id>/), and register any friction as debt in docs/planning/DEBT.md. A PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS verdict requires operator approval (gate H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION) before execution continues.
acceptance_tests: Framework baseline generated
Preflight verdict recorded
Evidence stored by path
Friction registered as debt

A preflight verdict artifact exists and validates against schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, and a baseline artifact exists and validates against schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json (both under .agent/, referenced by path)
Any recorded friction has a corresponding NDEBT-### row in docs/planning/DEBT.md (or an explicit 'no friction' note in the evidence)
A PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS verdict carries structured operator-approval metadata (gate H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION) before execution continues
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: [0-9]+ passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Dogfood Framework Audit + Delta (PR-F4)
features.description: Handover F-014 (Dogfood framework audit and delta). Produce at least one schema-valid engineering finding for nizam-framework, run a second baseline and compare it with the first (an audit delta proving unchanged facts stay stable, a deliberately changed fixture is detected, resolved findings close only with evidence, and stale evidence is not silently reused), and update docs/planning/ROADMAP.md from the dogfood evidence. Outputs: .agent/audits/<id>/findings.json and .agent/audits/<id>/report.md.
acceptance_tests: At least one finding validates
Second baseline compared with first
Roadmap updated from evidence
At least one .agent/audits/<id>/findings.json entry validates against schema/engineering_finding.schema.json
A second baseline exists and an audit delta artifact (.agent/audits/<id>/report.md) distinguishes new/resolved/reopened/stale findings and traces score movement to evidence
docs/planning/ROADMAP.md was updated from the dogfood evidence (a dated entry referencing the framework audit/delta): grep -Eqi 'dogfood|audit|ecosystem' docs/planning/ROADMAP.md
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: [0-9]+ passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Release Framework Capability — HUMAN GATE (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE)
features.description: Handover F-015 (Release framework capability). Prepare the framework release of the ecosystem cycle: classify the version correctly (expected MINOR per methodology/06_release_train.md — additive module/schemas/tooling, no breaking runtime change), update CHANGELOG.md and the user guide, obtain human sign-off, and publish an immutable tag. This is a HUMAN GATE (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE): the pipeline prepares and records the release but does NOT self-cut the tag or self-approve. Release exit criteria (validation green, dogfood complete, no unresolved P0/P1 in the new capability) must hold first.
acceptance_tests: Version classified correctly
Changelog and guide updated
Human sign-off complete
Immutable tag published

CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] (or the release section) names the ecosystem-cycle deliverables and the classified version: python3 -c "import re,sys; t=open('CHANGELOG.md',encoding='utf-8').read().lower(); sys.exit(0 if 'ecosystem' in t else 1)"
The release tag is NOT auto-cut by the pipeline (human gate): the release is recorded but the tag creation and sign-off are performed by a human with release authority
Release exit criteria hold: compliance validator green and no unresolved P0/P1 in the new capability recorded as Open in docs/planning/DEBT.md
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: [0-9]+ passed, 0 failed'
